About: It is about the hermit Saint Onuphrius.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Emmanuel Tzanes.
It is associated with the Late Cretan School.
It is associated with the Heptanese School of painting.
It is associated with Flemish Engravings.
It is associated with the movement that introduced Flemish Engravings into the Greek world.
It is associated with the Greek Monastery of Varlaam.
It is associated with Frangos Katelanos.
It is associated with Francisco Collantes.
It is associated with Jusepe de Ribera.
It is associated with Lorenzo Lotto.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Late Cretan School.
It belongs to the Heptanese School of painting.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Emmanuel Tzanes.
ConsistsOf: It consists of egg tempera paint.
CreatedBy: It was created by Emmanuel Tzanes.
HasA: It has a thickness of 1.7 cm.
It has a massive art catalog attributed to Emmanuel Tzanes.
It has a scroll with a Greek inscription.
It has a palm tree growing out of the mountain.
It has a waterfall flowing from the mountain into a stream.
It has a landscape establishing a foreground, a middle ground, and a background.
It has a cave with an opening.
It has a dove present signifying the Holy Spirit.
It has Greek inscriptions.
It has a leafy perizoma or loincloth.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its humble and unpretentious facial expression and pose.
It is characterized by finely painted grey and white hair.
It is characterized by humanistic elderly wrinkles on the face, arms, and legs.
It is characterized by visible ribs.
It is characterized by visible knees and elbows.
It is characterized by a three-dimensional appearance.
It is characterized by the evolution of the Cretan School.
It is characterized by the mannerisms of the Heptanese school.
It is characterized by the introduction of Flemish Engravings into the Greek world.
It is characterized by the humanization of figures and attention to human anatomy.
HeldIn: It was held in the collection of Rena Andreadis in Athens Greece.
It was held at the Benaki Museum.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the mannerisms of the Heptanese school.
It was influenced by the movement that introduced Flemish Engravings into the Greek world.
IsA: It is an emblem of the evolution of the Cretan School.
It is a depiction of Saint Onuphrius.
It is a work of Emmanuel Tzanes.
It is considered one of the painter's mature works.
KnownFor: It is known for its depiction of Saint Onuphrius.
It is known for being a mature work by Emmanuel Tzanes.
It is known for its representation of the Late Cretan School and Heptanese School.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the desert of Upper Egypt.
It occurred in the desert for over sixty years.
It occurred in isolation for over seventy years.
PartOf: It is part of the collection of Rena Andreadis in Athens Greece.
UsedAs: It is used as a depiction of Saint Onuphrius.
It is used as an emblem of the evolution of the Cretan School.
It is used as a representation of the mannerisms of the Heptanese school.
UsedFor: It is used for artistic representation.
UsedIn: It is used in the collection of Rena Andreadis in Athens Greece.
